stories.title: Weekend Family Roundup — Things to Do with Kids in Colorado Springs July 25–27
stories.angle: MANDATORY THURSDAY ROUNDUP — Best picks for families with kids in Colorado Springs and the Pikes Peak region this Friday through Sunday. Leads: Bronc Day Festival (free, Green Mountain Falls, Saturday July 25), Sunday Market at Acacia Park (free, Sunday July 26), Cheyenne Mountain Zoo (centennial year, open daily), Garden of the Gods (always free), Music in the Park at Piñon Valley (free, Friday July 25). Article published to springsdaily.com via parenting-ingest bridge.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
COS families, here's your weekend guide 👇 Bronc Day Festival in Green Mountain Falls (Sat, free all day), Sunday Market at Acacia Park (Sun 9 AM–2 PM, free), and Garden of the Gods all weekend.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The 88th Bronc Day Festival is Saturday in Green Mountain Falls — pancake breakfast at 7:30 AM, parade at 10 AM, live music and pie contest at Gazebo Lake Park. Free admission, 20 min west of COS on US-24.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Your Colorado Springs weekend just got a little easier to plan. 🗓️

Here are the best picks for families this weekend, July 24–26 — and most of them are completely free.

**Bronc Day Festival** is the anchor pick. Saturday, July 25, in Green Mountain Falls — about 20 minutes west of Colorado Springs on US-24. The 88th annual festival runs 7:30 AM to 3 PM and includes a pancake breakfast at Church in the Wildwood (7:30–10 AM), a parade down Ute Pass Avenue at 10 AM, and a full day of live music, family activities, and a pie contest at Gazebo Lake Park. Free admission for everyone.

**Music in the Park at Piñon Valley** gets the Friday evening slot — 6:30 to 8:30 PM at Piñon Valley Park (5585 Mule Deer Dr.), with live music, kickball, basketball, and food trucks. Free.

**Sunday Market at Acacia Park** rounds out the weekend on Sunday, July 26, from 9 AM to 2 PM in downtown Colorado Springs. Fresh produce, local goods, live music, and an easy stroll through Acacia Park. Also free.

Garden of the Gods is always open sunrise to sunset, always free, and this Saturday features a guided Siamese Twins Trail hike at 9 AM — a great way to see the formations with a ranger on hand.

Find the full list of things to do with kids in Colorado Springs this weekend at springsdaily.com, including the best free options and everything else happening around the Pikes Peak region.

What does your family have planned this weekend? Drop it in the comments 👇
facebook.image_caption:
Weekend plans: Bronc Day Festival Saturday in Green Mountain Falls 🎪 Free admission, pancake breakfast, parade, pie contest — all day, all ages. Plus Sunday Market, Garden of the Gods, and more free picks for COS families this weekend.

What's first on your list this weekend?

article.headline: Things to Do with Kids in Colorado Springs This Weekend (July 24–26)

stories.title: Safety Alert — Boon PIVOT Toddler Tower Recalled for Tip-Over Risk
stories.angle: CPSC RECALL — TOMY is recalling 116,908 Boon PIVOT Collapsible Toddler Tower Kitchen Step Stools (announced July 16, 2026) due to tip-over and fall hazards. The gray-and-white plastic and metal towers — about 22 inches wide, 23 inches deep, 35 inches tall — were sold at Target, Amazon, and Babylist from January 2023 through June 2026 for about $150. There have been 11 reports of instability, no injuries. Stop using immediately; store out of children's reach. TOMY will send a free stabilizing repair part. Register at recall.tomy.com/pivot.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
RECALL: TOMY recalls 116,908 Boon PIVOT Toddler Towers for tip-over risk. Sold at Target, Amazon, Babylist Jan 2023–Jun 2026 (~$150). Stop use immediately. TOMY will send a free repair part. recall.tomy.com/pivot

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Heads up for Colorado Springs families: if you have a Boon PIVOT Collapsible Toddler Tower in your kitchen, stop using it now. ⚠️

TOMY announced a recall on July 16 covering 116,908 of these kitchen step stools after 11 reports of the units becoming unstable, shifting, or leaning while in use. No injuries have been reported, but the tip-over and fall risk is real — and these are used by toddlers standing at counter height.

The recalled towers are gray and white, roughly 22 inches wide, 23 inches deep, and 35 inches tall. They were sold at Target, Amazon, and Babylist from January 2023 through June 2026 for around $150.

The good news: TOMY is handling the remedy through a free stabilizing repair part, not a return or a refund. Register your tower at recall.tomy.com/pivot and they'll send the part to you.

In the meantime, stop using the tower and store it somewhere out of your kids' reach.

Do you have a Boon PIVOT tower at home?
facebook.image_caption:
RECALL: 116,908 Boon PIVOT Toddler Towers recalled for tip-over risk. Stop using now. Register at recall.tomy.com/pivot for a free repair part from TOMY.

Has anyone else dealt with a kitchen step stool recall before?

article.headline: TOMY Recalls 116,908 Boon PIVOT Toddler Towers Over Tip-Over and Fall Hazards

stories.title: Bronc Day Festival — Green Mountain Falls' Beloved Summer Tradition Is Saturday
stories.angle: LOCAL EVENT — The 88th annual Bronc Day Festival returns to Green Mountain Falls (20 minutes west of Colorado Springs on US-24) on Saturday July 25, 7:30 AM–3 PM. This year's theme is "Let Freedom Ring!" honoring the USA's 250th anniversary and Colorado's 150th. Free admission. Events include: pancake breakfast 7:30–10 AM at Church in the Wildwood (10585 Ute Pass Ave); parade at 10 AM on Ute Pass Ave; family activities, live music, and a pie contest at Gazebo Lake Park (100 Lake St). Grand Marshal: retired Colorado Springs Fire Chief Steve Murphy. Free and family-friendly for all ages.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The 88th annual Bronc Day Festival is Saturday in Green Mountain Falls — free, pancake breakfast at 7:30 AM, parade at 10 AM, family activities all day at Gazebo Lake Park. About 20 min west of COS on US-24.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Green Mountain Falls is throwing a party this Saturday — and you're invited. 🏔️

The 88th annual Bronc Day Festival runs from 7:30 AM to 3 PM on Saturday, July 25, with free admission and enough going on to fill a whole family morning.

The day kicks off with a pancake breakfast at Church in the Wildwood (10585 Ute Pass Ave.) from 7:30 to 10 AM — a community tradition that draws people from all over the Pikes Peak region. At 10 AM, the parade steps off on Ute Pass Avenue with this year's Grand Marshal, retired Colorado Springs Fire Chief Steve Murphy.

After the parade, the action moves to Gazebo Lake Park (100 Lake St.) for family activities, live music, vendors, and the annual pie contest through 3 PM.

This year's theme is "Let Freedom Ring" — a nod to both the U.S. 250th anniversary and Colorado's 150th. Green Mountain Falls is about 20 minutes west of Colorado Springs on US-24.

Have you been to Bronc Day before? What's your favorite part?
facebook.image_caption:
The 88th Bronc Day Festival is Saturday in Green Mountain Falls 🎉 Pancake breakfast at 7:30 AM, parade at 10 AM, live music and pie contest all day at Gazebo Lake Park. Free admission, all ages. About 20 min west of COS on US-24.

Are you heading out to Green Mountain Falls this Saturday?

article.headline: Bronc Day Festival Returns Saturday to Green Mountain Falls for Its 88th Year

stories.title: Safety Alert — Target Cat & Jack Toddler Sandals Recalled for Choking Hazard
stories.angle: CPSC RECALL — Target is recalling about 211,000 pairs of Cat & Jack Toddler Girls' Sequerah Sandals (July 16, 2026) after decorative plastic pearls were found to detach from the tan raffia sandals, posing a choking hazard. Sold in Target stores and at Target.com from January through May 2026 for about $20. Target has received 23 reports of pearls coming loose. Stop using immediately; return to any Target store for a full refund.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
RECALL: Target is recalling ~211,000 Cat & Jack Toddler Girls' Sequerah Sandals — plastic pearls can detach, choking hazard. Sold Jan–May 2026 for ~$20. Return to any Target for a full refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If your toddler has worn the tan Cat & Jack sandals with the little decorative pearls, it's time to check your shoe bin. 👟

Target is recalling about 211,000 pairs of Cat & Jack Toddler Girls' Sequerah Sandals after 23 reports of the plastic pearls detaching from the shoe. Detached plastic pearls are a choking hazard for young kids, and these sandals were popular — sold at Target stores and Target.com from January through May 2026 for about $20.

The recall was announced July 16. The sandals are described as tan raffia with decorative plastic pearls.

The remedy is straightforward: stop using the sandals, and return them to any Target store for a full refund. No need to call ahead or mail them in — just bring them back.

Did you pick these up earlier this year?
facebook.image_caption:
RECALL: ~211,000 pairs of Cat & Jack Toddler Girls' Sequerah Sandals are being recalled. Decorative plastic pearls can detach — choking hazard. Sold Jan–May 2026 at Target for ~$20. Return to any Target for a full refund. ⚠️

Do you have these at home?

stories.title: CMZoo Turns 100 — A Centennial Summer at Colorado Springs' Favorite Zoo
stories.angle: LOCAL FEATURE — Cheyenne Mountain Zoo (4250 Cheyenne Mountain Zoo Rd) is celebrating its 100th anniversary in 2026, making this summer a milestone visit for Colorado Springs families. The zoo is open daily 9 AM–5 PM (last admission 4 PM); advance timed tickets required (check cmzoo.org for 2026 pricing). In 2026 the zoo is sharing centennial stories and running a Historic Photo Re-Creation Contest (ages 18+) for families to engage with CMZoo history. The zoo sits at 6,800 feet elevation — the only accredited mountain zoo in the US — and is home to more than 750 animals. Good for all ages; recommended for 2 and up.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Cheyenne Mountain Zoo is celebrating its 100th anniversary this summer — Colorado's only accredited mountain zoo, 750+ animals at 6,800 feet. Open daily 9 AM–5 PM. Advance tickets required. Check cmzoo.org

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you haven't visited Cheyenne Mountain Zoo yet this summer, this is the year to make it happen. 🦒

CMZoo is celebrating its 100th anniversary in 2026 — a genuine milestone for what's become one of the most distinctive zoos in the country. It's the only accredited mountain zoo in the United States, perched at 6,800 feet with more than 750 animals representing 170+ species.

In its centennial year, the zoo has been sharing the stories behind its 100-year history and running a Historic Photo Re-Creation Contest (ages 18 and up) where families can connect with the zoo's past in a hands-on way.

The zoo is open daily from 9 AM to 5 PM, with last admission at 4 PM. Advance timed tickets are required for both members and non-members — check cmzoo.org for 2026 pricing and availability before you head out.

CMZoo is located at 4250 Cheyenne Mountain Zoo Rd., Colorado Springs. Recommended for ages 2 and up.

Have you taken your kids to CMZoo this summer? What's their favorite animal?
facebook.image_caption:
Cheyenne Mountain Zoo is celebrating 100 years in 2026 🎂 Colorado's only accredited mountain zoo, 750+ animals at 6,800 feet. Open daily 9 AM–5 PM. Advance tickets required — check cmzoo.org.

What's your family's favorite animal at CMZoo?

article.headline: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo Marks Its 100th Year — A Centennial Summer Worth Visiting
